tweets.text:
Specialized capabilities and tireless vigilance across 31k+ miles of maritime borders.

In 4 separate cases last week, #USCG crews from Cutter David Duren, Station San Diego, and Maritime Safety and Security Team San Francisco interdicted 41 aliens attempting illegal entry near San Diego. During multiple pursuits, our crews used advanced tactics to intercept non-compliant vessels and stop human smuggling, in close partnership with U.S. Border Patrol.

A vigilant Coast Guard means a safer nation.

@USCGSouthwest @DHSgov @CBP

tweets.text:
As Artemis II reaches for the moon, maritime dominance has never been so important.

With NASA’s launch of Artemis II heralding America’s return to crewed lunar exploration, the USCG was on station, executing our critical role in this national endeavor.

In seamless partnership with @NASA and interagency partners, our crews ensured the safety and security of the maritime domain, demonstrating our unique capability to support complex interagency missions.

Congratulations to NASA and the crew of Artemis II on a successful launch!

Read more here: https://t.co/6DueyPj5Ix

@USCGSoutheast

tweets.text:
We’re always ready for the call. But when you’re miles from shore and disaster strikes, how will we find you?

On #406Day, we remind all mariners that a properly registered 406 MHz Emergency Position Indicating Radio Beacon (EPIRB) is your last, best hope at taking the “search” out of search and rescue.

Check your registration. Check your battery. Stay safe.

tweets.text:
Sustained impact through Operation Pacific Viper. 

@USCG Cutter Escanaba and its embarked helicopter team located and recovered a floating bale field Sunday in the Eastern Pacific, seizing over 4,500 lbs of cocaine valued at nearly $34M. That’s 1.7M potentially lethal doses that will never reach our communities. 

Through Operation Pacific Viper, the Coast Guard has seized 215K+ pounds of cocaine and apprehended 160 suspected narco-terrorists. Alongside our partners and allies, we’re scouring the Eastern Pacific, disrupting criminal networks, and keeping America safe. 

Bravo Zulu, CGC Escanaba! 

Read more here: https://t.co/LIugIaySpe

tweets.text:
Five lives saved. 

On April 2, @USCG Cutter Waesche, MH-60 Jayhawk, and HC-130 Hercules aircrews rescued five people from fishing vessel Ocean Bay after it grounded and took on water near Umnak Island.  

A Good Samaritan vessel arrived first on scene and relayed vital communications before our crews arrived and safely hoisted all five mariners from the vessel. 

This is integrated search and rescue in action. On the worst of days, in the harshest environments, we're Always Ready. 

Read more: https://t.co/P5rhy5CRxf

tweets.text:
On National Medal of Honor Day, we honor the legacy of our sole recipient, Signalman 1st Class Douglas Munro.

On September 27, 1942, he made the ultimate sacrifice at Guadalcanal, using his boat to shield 500 Marines from enemy fire. Douglas Munro’s selfless actions and unwavering commitment to his shipmates continue to inspire every member of the #USCG to this day. His heroism, for which he posthumously received the Medal of Honor, exemplified the Coast Guard’s sacred trust with the American people and our bond with our fellow armed services.

@DHSgov 

#SemperParatus

tweets.text:
How did #USCG Cutter Polar Star celebrate its 50th birthday? By breaking a cruise ship free from Antarctic ice.

This was just one moment in the successful 55-day Operation Deep Freeze. The Polar Star’s crew also carved a 7-mile channel, enabling the delivery of millions of gallons of fuel and a new pier for the U.S. Antarctic Program. As the United States' only heavy polar icebreaker, the Polar Star's performance underscores its indispensable role in maintaining strategic year-round access to the polar regions. This is global impact – facilitating commerce and defending America’s national interests in these critical and challenging environments.

After 14,000 miles, the crew is heading home. Bravo Zulu on a historic deployment!

Read more: https://t.co/0qIyZ92in2

#SemperParatus

tweets.text:
Commemorating a Hero’s Final Flight.

Yesterday, the Coast Guard honored the life and service of Aviation Survival Technician Second Class (AST2) Tyler Jaggers. Following a ceremony at Joint Base Lewis-McChord in Tacoma, Washington, a C-130 airplane transported AST2 Jaggers and his family to Joint Base Andrews for a Dignified Arrival ceremony. In the presence of the Secretary of Homeland Security, Commandant of the Coast Guard, Coast Guard servicemembers, and loved ones, the Ceremonial Honor Guard received AST2 Jaggers’ casket and rendered honors.

Through his selfless service and sacrifice, AST2 Jaggers represented the very best of our Service and the Aviation Rescue Swimmer community. As we solemnly mark AST2 Jaggers’ final flight, we will continue to honor his extraordinary heroism, and his devotion to the Coast Guard’s sacred trust: To save others.

#USCG #SoOthersMayLive

@SecMullinDHS @DHSgov @USCGPACAREA @USCGNorthwest

author.description: Official U.S. Coast Guard X account - The Nation’s premier maritime military service and law enforcement agency. If in distress, use VHF channel 16 or dial 911.

tweets.text:
Two years after the tragic collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge, we pause to honor the lives lost and remember the resilience of the Baltimore community. 

From the first moments, @USCG coordinated a complex response alongside our federal, state, and local counterparts. Bringing a range of authorities and capabilities to bear, we established a unified command, deployed advanced underwater sonar drones to search for victims in hazardous conditions, and guided the monumental effort to safely clear the 700-foot Fort McHenry Federal Channel.  

The Key Bridge collapse underscored the significance of America’s Marine Transportation System, our vital network of ports and waterways. As efforts continue toward rebuilding the Key Bridge, the Coast Guard is steadfast in our resolve to respond to contingencies and facilitate commerce vital to economic prosperity.

tweets.text:
Responding in Crisis. Anywhere. Anytime.

Watch as our MH-60T Jayhawk Aircrew from @USCG Air Station Borinquen hoists 2 people to safety from the bottom of a shoreside cliff in Fajardo, Puerto Rico last Saturday. The survivors, an 18-year-old woman and a 19-year-old man, were stranded after attempting to climb the cliff because of a worsening sea state.

This is our sacred trust in action: Answering the call, working seamlessly with local partners, and saving lives in the most challenging environments.

Read more: https://t.co/sEqPOG32Jv

#USCG #AlwaysReady #SoOthersMayLive
@DHSgov @USCGSoutheast

On Tuesday, @USCG Cutter Tampa seized 1,700+ lbs of cocaine worth an estimated $12.8 million, and apprehended 10 suspects from a drug-smuggling vessel in the Eastern Pacific.

tweets.text:
Layered security against unlawful maritime migration.
 
Working with our partners @CBP and @USMC, the crew of #USCG Cutter Thetis interdicted an overloaded, makeshift vessel Friday in the Mona Passage near Puerto Rico. We safely embarked and transferred the 60 people aboard to the Dominican Republic Navy for repatriation.

Our message is clear: illegal migrant voyages are dangerous, and you will be repatriated.

Always ready, the men and women of the Coast Guard remain on watch across our maritime borders and approaches, where defense of America begins.

Read more: https://t.co/X5bC0UWB0W

#USCG #WeOwnTheSea #SemperParatus #MaritimeDominance
@DHSgov

tweets.text:
Welcome home, CGC Munro!

After a historic 119-day, 26,000-mile patrol, the Munro’s crew returned to Alameda, California following the single largest maritime drug seizure in 18 years.

Through Operation Pacific Viper, they interdicted over 22,000 lbs of cocaine worth an estimated $250M, a record for our HITRON teams that keeps drugs off our streets. Spearheading the nation’s efforts to interdict dark fleet oil tankers, CGC Munro’s shadowed the Motor Tanker Bella 1 across the Atlantic, culminating in the vessel’s seizure.

Unmatched impact in defense of America by a dedicated crew. Read more: https://t.co/wDSjjv7Jr1

tweets.text:
Carving a path through the Antarctic ice.

#USCG Cutter Polar Star is on station for Operation Deep Freeze, clearing the way for vital resupply ships to reach McMurdo Station.

Ever wonder how we navigate the ice so efficiently? The Coast Guard is accelerating our use of advanced robotic and autonomous systems, like the SkyDio X10D, to survey ice conditions, enhancing safety, vigilance, and mission effectiveness.

In some of the world’s most challenging environments, like the Antarctic, we’re unifying vigilance and innovation to empower our crews and secure U.S. interests.

@DHSgov @USCGPACAREA 

#SemperParatus

Relentless vigilance. Unifying leadership. Coordinated response.

In response to catastrophic flooding in Oahu, @USCG and @USNavy aircrews rescued seven people and one dog in separate incidents yesterday in Waialua and Yokohama Bay.

Alongside our federal, state, and local partners, the Coast Guard is bringing every capability to bear - cutters, boats, aircraft, and crisis leaders - as this response evolves.

As America’s maritime first responder, the Coast Guard stands with the people of Oahu.

Read more here: https://t.co/KRvJLG1AAE

tweets.text:
When a mayday call comes, every second counts. 

On March 11, Station Boothbay Harbor responded to a sinking vessel 23 miles off the coast of Maine. The crew arrived on scene to find three mariners in the water.  

Within one minute, all three were safely aboard the USCG’s rescue boat. 

For one of the rescued mariners, the thought of his family fueled his will to survive. "I have a newborn baby, and I was thinking about getting home to my newborn,” he later shared. 

Outstanding work by our rescuers and supporting aircrews. As America’s maritime first responder, the Coast Guard is Always Ready to respond in crisis. 

Read more: https://t.co/yIbNZrohdT

#SemperParatus

tweets.text:
Presidents’ Day is a powerful reminder of how U.S. presidents have led, empowered, and inspired the U.S. Coast Guard to serve the American people across our missions. 

From Woodrow Wilson’s 1915 Act, to FDR’s WWII expansions, to unprecedented modernization and investment under President Trumps’ One Big Beautiful Bill Act, presidential decisions have guided the Coast Guard through historic moments to make us the world’s premier maritime fighting force. 

Whether controlling U.S. borders and maritime approaches, facilitating maritime commerce, or responding to contingencies, the Coast Guard remains on watch and Always Ready to meet the Nation’s most urgent needs.

#PresidentsDay #USCG #SemperParatus

tweets.text:
Stopping narco-terrorism from the Caribbean to the Eastern Pacific.

After surpassing 200K+ lbs of cocaine seized through Operation Pacific Viper, @USCG has surged counter-drug operations into the next gear. Last week, Coast Guard Cutter Seneca offloaded 17,750+ lbs of cocaine seized in the Eastern Pacific, and crews at Station Fort Lauderdale seized 745 lbs from 2 vessels off Port Everglades.

80% of U.S.-bound drug interdictions occur at sea vice land. That’s our finest maritime fighting force, defending U.S. borders and maritime approaches and keeping deadly drugs out of our communities.

Read more here: https://t.co/8F3L5uPPta

#USCG #OperationPacificViper #MaritimeSecurity #HomelandSecurity

tweets.text:
Honoring our sacred trust with the American people.

When historic floods devastated Central Texas this July, @USCG Aviation Survival Technician Scott Ruskan and his fellow crewmembers answered the call.

Last night, @POTUS awarded Ruskan the Legion of Merit during the State of the Union address for rescuing nearly 170 people, including Camp Mystic children. His heroic efforts underscored our promise to American people. In the face of unspeakable tragedy, we stand Always Ready to save lives, as we did for over 5,200 people last year.

#USCG #SOTU #CampMystic 
@DHSgov @USCGHeartland

tweets.text:
In the late 1990s, maritime interdiction rates for narcotics were below 10 percent due to high-speed smuggling vessels. In response, the U.S. Coast Guard formed an experimental aviation unit, pioneering Airborne Use of Force. The Helicopter Interdiction Tactical Squadron’s success fundamentally changed counter-narcotic strategy.

We’re proud of HITRON’s 1,000+ drug interdictions. Find out what goes into each one. Monday, we’ll share a deep-dive of this game-changing mission against narco-terrorists.

#USCG #HITRON #MaritimeSecurity #DrugInterdiction
@DHSgov

Icebreaking is about precision. Every operation in frozen waterways is deliberate—driven by timing, judgment, and coordination in some of the most demanding operating conditions. From the Great Lakes to the Northeast to the Mid-Atlantic, @USCG crews continuously assess ice behavior, vessel movement, and environmental factors to ensure safe, reliable access where it’s needed most. As we modernize our domestic icebreaker fleet, we continue to facilitate commerce in response to ice impacts to navigation on our waterways. 

Read more here: https://t.co/Qp5MeALHHO

tweets.text:
A decisive move to guarantee American security in the Arctic.

Today, the Coast Guard announced it has completed the award of contracts for 11 Arctic Security Cutters, fulfilling President Trump’s directive to rapidly expand America’s icebreaker fleet. The latest award is for construction of up to five Arctic Security Cutters, bringing the President’s initial order to completion and marking a historic milestone in U.S. Arctic capability. Davie Defense, Inc. will construct two Arctic Security Cutters at Helsinki Shipyard in Finland and three domestically.

These cutters will form the backbone of a revitalized U.S. icebreaker fleet, protecting U.S. sovereignty and dominance in the Arctic.

Delivery of the first vessel is expected in 2028, marking a significant step in service’s modernization through Force Design 2028. 

Read more here: https://t.co/Teo2G1aYVN

#USCG #ForceDesign2028 #Arctic #OBBB @DHSgov @POTUS @Sec_Noem

tweets.text:
The United States Coast Guard recently apprehended a stowaway smuggler aboard a tug vessel and seized 10 bales of cocaine, with a wholesale value of more than $5 million, in San Juan Harbor, Puerto Rico, during a combined operation with @DHSGov’s Homeland Security Task Force - San Juan Region.

As both a law enforcement agency and military branch, we are uniquely positioned to defend the Homeland and secure our maritime borders against criminal organizations and narco-terrorists. Alongside joint and interagency partners, Coast Guard men and women are increasing operations to interdict, seize, and disrupt transshipment of cocaine and other bulk illicit drugs by sea.

Read more at: https://t.co/efkhMVVGyR

#USCG #Interdiction #MaritimeSecurity

tweets.text:
A ready @USCG starts with a healthy workforce.

Through Force Design 2028, we’re forging a more agile, capable, and responsive maritime fighting force. To accelerate that effort, the Service is launching the Physical Readiness Program, to include mandated daily physical training and body composition compliance.

Nutrition is a key element. Senior Chief Sonata Haley shares practical nutrition tips that support energy, focus, and resilience, because readiness begins with taking care of our people.

Proper nutrition means you won’t just be fit for duty, you’ll be fit for life.

#USCG #ForceDesign2028 #Readiness

tweets.text:
Elite capabilities. Unmatched authorities. Unified resolve.
 
This morning, a @USCG tactical team boarded and seized Motor Tanker Veronica, a sanctioned oil tanker, in the Caribbean Sea. Our unique maritime law enforcement authorities – and thorough interagency coordination with the Departments of War, Justice, and State – ensured a safe, seamless operation in accordance with U.S. and international law.
 
Alongside U.S. Sailors and Marines, our maritime fighting force is bringing every capability at our disposal to interdict ghost fleet tankers and uphold international law. With unwavering resolve, the Coast Guard continues operations to control, secure, and defend our U.S. borders and maritime approaches. We stand Always Ready to defend the American people from threats on the high seas.

Through Force Design 2028, the Coast Guard is delivering unprecedented return on America’s investment.

Over the past year, the Coast Guard seized 554,.000 pounds of illegal narcotics, interdicted 11,173 illegal aliens, saved 4,946 lives, saved or assisted over $974 million in property, and ensured the safe movement of 1.8 billion tons of maritime cargo — measurable outcomes across border control, flow of commerce, and contingency response.

By rapidly applying reconciliation funding to operational capability and empowering operators at the tactical edge through expanded authorities, Force Design 2028 is strengthening readiness and execution across operations like Pacific Viper and Trident Forge.

This is Force Design 2028 delivering results.

Read more here: https://t.co/p0tzTdJPbt

#USCG #ForceDesign2028 @DHSgov

Over the past year, the Coast Guard seized 554,.000 pounds of illegal narcotics, interdicted 11,173 illegal aliens, saved 4,946 lives, saved or assisted over $974 million in property, and ensured the safe movement of 1.8 billion tons of maritime cargo — measurable outcomes across border control, flow of commerce, and contingency response.

By rapidly applying reconciliation funding to operational capability and empowering operators at the tactical edge through expanded authorities, Force Design 2028 is strengthening readiness and execution across operations like Pacific Viper and Trident Forge.

This is Force Design 2028 delivering results.

Read more here: https://www.news.uscg.mil/Press-Releases/Article/4380080/coast-guard-releases-force-design-2028-initial-update-highlights-return-on-inve/

#USCG #ForceDesign2028 @DHSgov

tweets.text:
Today, Adm. Kevin Lunday assumed command as the 28th Commandant of the U.S. Coast Guard.

Admiral Lunday takes command at a pivotal moment as the Coast Guard continues our transformation through Force Design 2028, powered by historic investment from the American people. Under his leadership, the Service will continue that return on investment while conducting operations to control, secure, and defend U.S. borders and maritime approaches; facilitate commerce vital to economic prosperity, strategic mobility, and U.S. maritime dominance; and respond to crises and contingencies that may come with little to no warning.

Read more here: https://t.co/SpbNmBd9zW

#USCG #SemperParatus @DHSgov

tweets.text:
Meet @USCG’s newest capability in the fight against criminal organizations: Midnight Express 392785.
 
Continuing our expedited acquisition and innovation through Force Design 2028, the Coast Guard has acquired a CBP 39-foot Midnight Express. As part of a pursuit adaptive force package, this asset will complement new and existing capabilities to counter non-compliant vessels while executing non-compliant vessel pursuit and use of force tactics.
 
The Midnight Express provides superior capability and handling in congested or constrained waterways, enables sustained operations in higher sea states, and brings the power and redundancy needed for extended patrols and high‑risk interdictions. Alongside other capabilities the Coast Guard is bringing aboard, the Midnight Express provides a flexible, scalable package optimized for rapidly detecting, containing, and interdicting non‑compliant vessels in the maritime domain.
 
Powered by Force Design 2028 and historic investment, the Coast Guard is forging a more agile, capable, and responsive force. Midnight Express just kicked that into a higher gear.

#USCG #BorderSecurity #AlwaysReady @DHSgov @USCGSouthwest @USCGPACAREA

Threats on our southern border are evolving. @USCG is adapting and mobilizing an unprecedented response to meet the challenge.

Through Operation River Wall, our maritime fighting force is bringing centuries of tactical boat experience – coastal, offshore, and riverine – to control the border along 260 miles of the Rio Grande.

Proud to be owning the rivers and securing our southern border alongside U.S. Border Patrol and U.S. Northern Command.

#USCG #WeOwnTheRivers #OperationRiverWall @DHSgov @USCGHeartland @USNorthernCmd @USCGLANTAREA

tweets.text:
Medical readiness is operational readiness.
 
Aboard Coast Guard Cutter Stone, Health Services Technicians ensure the crew remains medically ready to operate during sustained missions at sea. CGC Stone set drug interdiction records through Operation Pacific Viper, offloading over 49,000 pounds of cocaine following a patrol in the Eastern Pacific. Petty Officer Harrison ensured the crew was ready to perform, respond, and remain on mission.
 
Being Always Ready depends on every crew member. Become part of the Semper Paratus legacy. Join the Coast Guard ➡️ https://t.co/J4bAetd2oX

tweets.text:
Behind every successful patrol is a crew that keeps the cutter operational.

Aboard @USCG Cutter Stone, Machinery Technicians keep propulsion, power, and critical systems operating to ensure the cutter remains ready to operate and respond. Through Operation Pacific Viper, CGC Stone and other @USCG units have interdicted over 170K pounds of cocaine since early August. That took a total team effort. As a Machinery Technician, Smallboat Engineer, and Boarding Team Member, Petty Officer Fair ensured CGC Stone was always ready to operate and deliver results wherever the mission took the crew.

What can you bring to being Semper Paratus? Find out at https://t.co/J4bAetcuzp

Powerful authorities. Unifying leadership. Unmatched impact.
 
Using its unique law enforcement authorities, @USCG tactical forces led a unified interagency effort to board and seize the Motor Tanker M Sophia east of the Caribbean Sea early this morning. Through close coordination with @DeptofWar and @StateDept, the Coast Guard conducted a safe, effective boarding consistent with international law.
 
This is the nation’s finest maritime fighting force – owning the sea and defending America.

#USCG #WeOwnTheSea
@DHSgov @USSOCOM

tweets.text:
Specialized capabilities. Global impact. An unstoppable joint force.
 
Alongside @DeptofWar, @USCG conducted a boarding and seizure of the Motor Tanker Bella I this morning in the North Atlantic. Following a sustained shadowing effort across the Atlantic by Coast Guard Cutter Munro, tactical Coast Guard teams leveraged our powerful maritime law enforcement authorities to secure Bella I through a tightly executed joint operation.
 
Through a whole of government effort and seamless coordination, our maritime fighting force is proud to own the sea, uphold international law, and defend America.

#USCG #WeOwnTheSea @DHSgov

tweets.text:
This morning, a @USCG tactical team, supported by @USMC and @USNavy , boarded and seized Motor Tanker (M/T) Olina in international waters east of the Caribbean. Interagency coordination, and an unstoppable joint force, were instrumental in executing a safe, carefully orchestrated operation consistent with international law.
 
Powered by unique law enforcement authorities and specialized capabilities, the Coast Guard is seizing sanctioned oil tankers and protecting the American people from threats on the high seas. M/T Olina is the latest example.
 
Bravo Zulu to our maritime fighting force – and our partners at @DeptofWar and @StateDept – for their relentless execution of this mission. The Coast Guard continues operations to control, secure, and defend U.S. borders and maritime approaches – where defense of America begins.
 
#USCG #WeOwnTheSea

tweets.text:
The Arctic demands access, power, and vigilance. @USCG delivers.

Through sustained efforts like Operation Frontier Sentinel, Coast Guard crews are protecting U.S. sovereignty, enhancing national security, and countering foreign malign influence in the Arctic. Following acquisition of Coast Guard Cutter Storis – the nation’s 1st icebreaker in over 25 years – we're bolstering our Arctic fleet through $7.8B+ in One Big Beautiful Bill funding for new icebreakers.

Continuing our return on America’s historic investment, the Coast Guard is ensuring access, controlling U.S. maritime borders and approaches, and delivering the operational advantage in the Arctic.

#USCG #Arctic #Polar #IceOperations @DHSgov @USCGArctic

tweets.text:
Happy Birthday, United States Space Force!

@USCG joins the Nation in marking the Space Force’s continued service in defense of U.S. interests and national security. In its first 6 years, the Space Force has cemented its role as America's sentinel in the high ground, securing our strategic advantage in a contested domain. This anniversary celebrates Space Force's unwavering commitment to space control, ensuring freedom of operation in space for both military capabilities and groundbreaking innovation.

The Coast Guard is proud to serve alongside our fellow military service, ready to meet evolving threats in defense of America.

#USCG #SpaceForce #USSF @USSpaceForce

Transforming our workforce. Reshaping our training capacity. 

Today, @USCG released a Request for Information in coordination with @DHSgov to identify possible locations for an additional training center. As the Coast Guard modernizes through Force Design 2028 and grows by up to 15,000 personnel, we need additional training capacity to shape a more agile, capable, and responsive maritime fighting force.

-Force Design 2028: https://t.co/XityiodE2w
-Training Center RFI: https://t.co/mtLrdqBeHK

tweets.text:
NEW SHIPS, TIMELESS LEGACY 

The Coast Guard is accelerating Waterways Commerce Cutter production with $162M in funding through the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. These modern ships will replace an aging fleet and keep America moving, maintaining 28,000+ aids to navigation and facilitating $5.4T in annual commerce. Designated as the “Chief Petty Officer Class,” these ships will proudly bear the names of Coast Guard Chief Petty Officers and honor their legacy as leaders, mentors, and technical experts. 

"Since 1920, Chief Petty Officers and the Chiefs Mess have driven Coast Guard readiness and operational excellence,” said Master Chief Petty Officer of the Coast Guard Phillip Waldron. “These new cutters and their crews will build on that legacy, ensuring maritime commerce flows safely and we continue to control, secure and defend our inland ports and waterways and Marine Transportation System.”

Read more here: https://t.co/j6him63eGC

#uscg #forcedesign2028 @DHSgov

A bold step forward for America and the U.S. Coast Guard.  

On August 10, we’ll commission the Storis (WAGB 21), our first polar icebreaker in 25+ years. This historic milestone strengthens U.S. Arctic presence, protects national security, and continues America’s leadership in the region.

The Storis is a game-changer for Arctic security, and will bolster the Coast Guard’s ability to control, secure, and defend the U.S. border around Alaska and maritime approaches in the Arctic.

Follow us for updates & an inside look at this historic milestone! #uscg #forcedesign2028 #arctic

tweets.text:
The United States Coast Guard has a new Master Chief Petty Officer of the Coast Guard. Master Chief Petty Officer Phillip N. Waldron relieved Master Chief Petty Officer Heath B. Jones in a Change of Watch ceremony in Alexandria, Va., today.  

Master Chief Waldron is the 15th Master Chief Petty Officer of the Coast Guard. A second-generation Coast Guardsman, he joined the Coast Guard in 1999 and most recently served as Command Master Chief at the Personnel Service Center. Master Chief Waldron’s distinguished career includes a range of afloat and ashore assignments. His extensive experience and proven senior leadership have prepared him to assume the highest senior enlisted leadership position in the Service.

As the Coast Guard transforms to a more agile, capable, and responsive force through Force Design 2028, Master Chief Waldron’s leadership, experience, and perspective will be invaluable.

The Coast Guard wishes Master Chief Jones and his family fair winds and following seas and thanks them for their service and dedication. #USCG #MCPOCG

tweets.text:
Meeting presence with presence. Yesterday, a @USCG aircraft responded following detection of a Chinese research vessel off Alaska’s coast.

For over 150 years, Americans have counted on the Coast Guard to uphold U.S. sovereignty, provide national security, and promote economic prosperity in the Arctic. Through Operation Frontier Sentinel, we continue to deliver on that commitment.

The Coast Guard is America’s only surface presence in the Arctic. A robust icebreaker fleet, made possible by historic investment in the Coast Guard, will secure U.S. access, security, and leadership in this growing zone of strategic global competition.
#USCG #ValuetoNation

Read more: https://t.co/J0lIyNOu12

tweets.text:
Leaner. More agile. More strategically focused.

Today, Senior Advisor to the Secretary for the Coast Guard Mr. Sean Plankey and Acting Commandant Admiral Kevin Lunday held a workforce town hall on Force Design 2028. As the first of four campaign-focused town halls, today’s session discussed Force Design 2028’s Organization Campaign, which will optimize our structure, hone coordination and decision-making, and better serve the workforce and the American people.

Changes are already underway. Learn more about Force Design and the way forward for our Organization Campaign: https://t.co/RIXmg5q95d

And tune in for the next town hall on Wednesday on the Contracting and Acquisitions Campaign.

#USCG #ForceDesign2028
@DHSgov

Bravo Zulu to the whole team! #USCG #druginterdiction

BREAKING: Acting Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Kevin Lunday & Senior Advisor to the Secretary of the Coast Guard Mr. Sean Plankey just wrapped the second of four campaign-focused town halls on Force Design 2028, providing an overview of the Contracting and Acquisitions campaign during today’s session with our crews. Here's what you need to know. 
 
FD 2028 isn't bureaucratic reshuffling; it's a complete structural overhaul maximizing taxpayer ROI. We're streamlining acquisitions, cutting red tape, and delivering critical assets faster and at lower cost. 
 
Bottom line: Our people get the tools they need, when they need them. That's how we serve America better. More on Contracting and Acquisitions under FD 2028: https://t.co/IXudtg9ZCP

#USCG #ForceDesign2028 @DHSgov

tweets.text:
Coast Guard Cutter (CGC) Storis’ historic voyage continues! Through the dedicated efforts of her crew, Storis arrived in Seattle earlier this week, having marked numerous milestones on its journey northward.

As the Nation’s third polar icebreaker, Storis was acquired to provide near-term operational presence, assure access to the polar regions to protect U.S. sovereignty, and support national security as a bridging strategy for Arctic presence. Embodying @USCG transformation through Force Design 2028, Storis will soon continue its transit to Juneau, Alaska for commissioning.

Don’t miss these latest photos of Storis’ voyage, including passing honors with CGC Eagle!
#USCG #Storis #PolarOperations #ForceDesign2028 #ArcticPresence #IcebreakerFleet

@USCGPACAREA @USCGArctic @DHSgov

tweets.text:
Busted! 🚫 These images show a drug boat’s desperate attempt to ditch its illicit cargo. Not this time—thanks to a satellite that detected suspicious activity and triggered the deployment of a @USCG drone.

As part of our Force Design 2028 transformation, Project Minerva is revolutionizing how we gather, share, and act on information across air, sea, land, and space. With smarter sensors, better connectivity, and integrated data systems, we’re shifting from “patrol and interdict” to “target and interdict.”

Continuing our return on America’s historic investment in the Coast Guard, we are modernizing our capabilities and exploiting technology at every turn to enhance mission execution and support. 

Learn more about how Project Minerva is revolutionizing maritime security here: https://t.co/yz1y0IUGet

#uscg #forcedesign2028 #maritimesecurity #innovation

@DHSgov

tweets.text:
Building the future force! Saturday, @USCG opened its newest recruiting office in Omaha, Nebraska.

As we modernize our assets and capabilities through historic investment, we are also modernizing how and where we recruit – ensuring we’re reaching the best talent in communities across the country. This office will strengthen our ability to build the most agile, capable, and responsive maritime fighting force.

Continuing our transformation through Force Design 2028, the Coast Guard has recruited over 5,200 new members this year, and expects to meet recruiting goals for the second year in a row.

Ready to be part of the Coast Guard of the future? Visit https://t.co/J4bAetd2oX to learn more. #uscg #forcedesign2028 #GoCoastGuard

tweets.text:
Every successful @USCG drug seizure begins with a team of experts: boarding teams, cutter crews, aviators, analysts, and personnel who keep our assets mission-ready. Building on our legacy of maritime drug interdiction, we employ an intelligence-driven process that integrates every capability at our disposal.

The result: 80% of all U.S.-bound narcotics are seized at sea. In just the past six months, the Coast Guard has confiscated more than 240,000 pounds of cocaine—stopping 91 million potentially lethal doses from reaching American communities and families.

Maritime interdiction is the most effective way to stop drugs before they reach our shores. Continued investment in our people, tools and operational reach is essential to disrupting transnational criminal organizations and protecting our communities.

Check out this graphic to learn more about the Coast Guard’s drug interdiction process. #USCG #DrugInterdiction
@DHSgov

Today marks the beginning of a new chapter for @USCG. A nearly $25 billion investment will transform the Service through modernization of assets, infrastructure, and technology. This funding supports Force Design 2028, Secretary Noem’s comprehensive effort to modernize operations and capabilities to ensure the Coast Guard remains the finest maritime fighting force ready to protect and defend the American people for decades to come.  
 
We extend our deep appreciation for the leadership of the Administration, including @POTUS  and @Sec_Noem, and leaders in Congress for recognizing the strategic importance of a ready, modern, and resilient Coast Guard.

Always ready, the men and women of the Coast Guard remain on watch across our maritime borders and approaches, where defense of America begins.

#USCG #ForceDesign2028 #ValuetoNation #EveryWarEveryShore
@DHSgov

tweets.text:
Value to Nation: Across the Coast Guard’s range of missions, our men and women are dedicated to keeping Americans safe and delivering on the Nation’s historic investment in the Service. We continue to surge resources to control, secure, and defend 31,000 miles of U.S. maritime borders — where defense of America begins.

We are combating foreign terrorist organizations, cartels, and transnational organized crime.

We facilitate commerce vital to economic security and strategic mobility.

We are always ready to respond to a crisis or contingency that may come with little or no warning.

Continuing our transformation through Force Design 2028, the Coast Guard remains focused on maximizing value to the Nation through the most agile, capable, and responsive maritime fighting force.

#USCG #CoastGuard #ValueToNation #SemperParatus #ForceDesign2028 @DHSgov

tweets.text:
A major milestone this week in the Coast Guard’s drug interdiction efforts: Since January 20, we’ve seized over 242,000 pounds of cocaine. With as little as 1.2g being enough to kill someone, that’s 91 million potentially lethal doses.  

@USCG continues to surge forces along the southern border and maritime approaches. The strategy is working. Applying our unique authorities and capabilities, we’ve stopped over 100% percent more cocaine than we did in 2024.

Bravo Zulu to the men and women of the Coast Guard. Through their tireless efforts – and close coordination with interagency partners – we are seizing record amounts of illegal drugs and making America safer. 
 
Read more: https://t.co/fW6ZUT4FOt

#USCG #druginterdiction #ValueToNation @DHSgov

tweets.text:
Two years ago, the Titan submersible tragedy claimed five lives and gripped the world.

A formal Marine Board of Investigation was initiated to fully understand what happened. The Coast Guard – along with our international and agency partners – will use those findings to help prevent future tragedies.

As we continue that work, we honor the five lives lost and extend our thoughts to their loved ones.

The Coast Guard’s priority was—and remains—ensuring safety at sea, both above and below the surface.
#uscg  #Titan  #MaritimeSafety #SearchAndRescue
@USCGLANTAREA

tweets.text:
The Coast Guard owns the sea. Continuing that vigilance – and our longstanding immigration enforcement mission – Coast Guard Cutter (CGC) Benjamin Bottoms (WPC-1132) interdicted 10 aliens aboard a 25-foot vessel in the vicinity of Point Loma. Through close coordination with Coast Guard Maritime Safety and Security Team Los Angeles/Long Beach and @CBP, CGC Benjamin Bottoms continued our efforts to detect, deter, and disrupt illeal maritime migration. 

The Coast Guard continues to surge assets to counter Transnational Criminal Organizations, drug smugglers, and human traffickers wherever they move. Through the tireless efforts of Coast Guard men and women, we are controlling, securing, and defending our maritime borders. Through the ongoing vigilance of Coast Guard men and women, we are making America safer. 

#CoastGuard #MaritimeSafety #IllegalMigration #Teamwork #PointLoma

tweets.text:
On this day in 1950, the Korean War began. The Coast Guard played an important role in the war effort by performing a range of missions including port security, search and rescue, and aids to navigation.  
 
Honoring the legacy of those who served, the Coast Guard continues our broad range of missions as the Nation’s finest military service. Across our borders, and on frontlines across the globe, Coast Guard men and women are safeguarding the homeland. Our members are always ready to keep the American people safe. 
 
Click here to learn more: https://t.co/pqNXbNcx6U

#USCG #AlwaysReady #FinestMilitaryService
@DHSgov

tweets.text:
U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Storis, the Service’s first polar icebreaker acquired in more than 25 years, departed on its maiden voyage to safeguard U.S. sovereign interests in the Arctic and conduct select Coast Guard missions.  As the Nation’s third polar icebreaker, Storis will be commissioned this August in Juneau, and then conduct a presence patrol in the Arctic. Storis’ departure marks an early milestone in the Service’s transformation through Force Design 2028, which includes reforming Coast Guard acquisitions to rapidly deploy capabilities to execute our missions.

Press Release: https://t.co/R3ZlT51fqF

#USCG #ForceDesign2028
@DHSgov

tweets.text:
Coast Guard crews around the country work diligently to prevent illegal narcotics from entering the United States.

Coast Guard Cutter Kimball exemplified these operations during a recent patrol from February through April, during which they conducted multiple drug interdictions off the coasts of Mexico and Central and South America. This culminated in Kimball offloading more than $191 million worth of cocaine in April.

As the Coast Guard continues to surge assets across our southern border and maritime approaches, our crews have been leading the fight against drug cartels and Transnational Criminal Organizations (TCO). The dedicated efforts of Kimball – and men and women across the Coast Guard – are directly impacting the ability of cartels and TCOs to smuggle illicit drugs into our communities. 

📷PA3 Avery Tibbets & MST3 Austin Wiley

@DHSgov @USCGPACAREA @USCGHawaiiPac 

#USCG #USCGHawaii #IndoPacific #druginterdiction

author.description: Official U.S. Coast Guard X account - The Nation’s premier maritime military service and law enforcement agency. If in distress, use VHF channel 16 or dial 911.

tweets.text:
Another significant win in our operations to disrupt criminal and terrorist organizations.

On May 22, 2025, the Coast Guard Investigative Service  – as part of the multi-agency Panama Express Strike Force – successfully extradited seven leaders of Transnational Criminal Organizations (TCOs) from Colombia. These individuals, which include suspects affiliated with Tren de Aragua and the Sinaloa Cartel, are accused of trafficking thousands of kilograms of narcotics through the U.S. southern border. These investigations were initiated and advanced by information obtained through criminal prosecutions resulting from Coast Guard maritime interdictions.

Each interdiction plays a vital role. This operation underscores the ongoing commitment and effectiveness of the Coast Guard and its interagency partners in protecting the homeland and combating transnational threats. From maritime interdictions to investigations to extraditions, the Coast Guard is directly impacting the ability of cartels and TCOs to smuggle illicit drugs into our communities.  #BorderSecurity #HSI #DEA #FBI #USCG #Partnerships  #CGIS

@DHSgov

tweets.text:
USCGC STRATTON WMSL 752 recently departed Puerto Princesa, Philippines, after a successful visit that reinforced the Coast Guard’s strong partnership with the Philippine Coast Guard.

During this inaugural port call, Capt. Brian Krautler hosted U.S. Ambassador MaryKay Carlson and PCG leadership, showcasing our commitment to maritime security and collaboration in the region.

Stratton’s crew engaged in crucial Maritime Cooperative Activities, including exercises in maritime domain awareness and search and seizure procedures, underscoring the Coast Guard’s dedication to freedom and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific.
@USCGPACAREA @coastguardph 

#USCG #CutterStratton #MaritimePartnership #IndoPacific #SecurityAndFreedom

Stratton’s crew engaged in crucial Maritime Cooperative Activities, including exercises in maritime domain awareness and search and seizure procedures, underscoring the Coast Guard’s dedication to freedom and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific.
@USCGPACAREA @coastguardph 

#USCG #CutterStratton #MaritimePartnership #IndoPacific #SecurityAndFreedom

author.description: Official U.S. Coast Guard X account - The Nation’s premier maritime military service and law enforcement agency. If in distress, use VHF channel 16 or dial 911.

tweets.text:
On April 30, 2025, the Coast Guard Investigative Service (CGIS) arrested a member of the Tren de Aragua (TdA) Foreign Terrorist Organization (FTO). Through close coordination with our federal law enforcement counterparts, CGIS capitalized on a Panama Express (PANEX) criminal investigation to arrest this TdA member pursuant to shipment of pre-cursors of fentanyl into Saint Petersburg, Florida.

This case underscores the value of maritime interdictions, and the impact of PANEX operations to investigate and arrest drug smugglers. Already, this arrest is informing subsequent PANEX operations to intercept drug shipments and locate other TdA members.

As CGIS surges resources to detect, deter, and interdict the trafficking of illicit drugs, including fentanyl, we are directly impacting the operations of cartels, FTOs, and Transnational Criminal Organizations. Working alongside our interagency counterparts, we are catching the worst of the worst and preventing illicit drugs from reaching our communities.
@DHSgov @TheJusticeDept @FBI @USCGSoutheast @DEAHQ 
#BorderSecurity #HSI #DEA #FBI #USCG #PARTNERSHIP #CGIS

tweets.text:
$23M in cocaine seized. Lives saved. Mission success.

During their 79 days in the Eastern Pacific Ocean, the crew of Coast Guard Cutter (CGC) Forward seized over $23 million in cocaine and intercepted a suspected drug-smuggling vessel.

The Coast Guard continues to surge resources to control, secure and defend our borders and maritime approaches. Leveraging our unique authorities and capabilities, CGC Forward has been at the leading edge of the Coast Guard’s efforts to detect, deter, and interdict illegal aliens, drug smugglers, and individuals intent on terrorism or other hostile activity before they reach our border.
#USCG  #Counternarcotics #MaritimeSecurity 
@USCGLANTAREA @DHSgov

tweets.text:
860 pounds. 19 bales. $13.65 million in cocaine. Stopped by one unified #team.

On May 25, the Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer USS Gravely (DDG 107) and a U.S. Coast Guard Law Enforcement Detachment (LEDET) seized an estimated 860 pounds of illegal drugs from a vessel in the #Caribbean Sea.

The #interdiction was conducted by Gravely’s Visit, Board, Search and Seizure team with an embarked Coast Guard #LEDET. The team boarded a vessel of interest and recovered 19 bales of #cocaine with an estimated value of $13.65 million.

Gravely operates under U.S. Northern Command’s maritime homeland defense authorities, with #LEDET personnel embarked to conduct interdiction missions targeting #illegaldrugs and other illicit maritime activity. With the LEDET’s tactical expertise guiding interdiction efforts, Gravely harnesses its advanced surveillance systems and mobility to locate and intercept suspect vessels, effectively extending Coast Guard authority through #navalpower to enhance #maritimesecurity operations. Together, the Navy’s mobility and the Coast Guard’s jurisdiction deliver a strong, coordinated response to threats offshore.

This is how sea power protects the #homeland.

#USNavy #USCG #MaritimeSecurity #JointForce #LEDET #DSF #USSGravely #DDG107
@USCGLANTAREA @USCGSoutheast @USNavy @DHSgov

tweets.text:
Across U.S. borders and maritime approaches, and throughout America’s ports and waterways, the Coast Guard continues its vigilance in securing the homeland.

During Fleet Week New York 2025, Maritime Safety and Security Team (MSST) New York (#91106) supported Sector New York’s maritime security operations throughout the port. Tactical boat crews enforced naval vessel protection zones, conducted maritime security and response patrols, secured key shoreside infrastructure, and kept the public safe.

Even as the #FleetWeekNYC crowds cleared up this week, MSST New York continued to protect critical ports, support interagency operations, and uphold the Coast Guard’s mission to control, secure, and defend our ports and waterways.

A vigilant Coast Guard means a safer nation.
#USCG #fleetweek2025 #SemperParatus
@USCGNortheast @DHSgov

tweets.text:
Maritime Safety and Security Team (MSST) Honolulu 91107 stands at the forefront of the Coast Guard’s mission to protect America’s interests.

Specializing in Ports, Waterways, and Coastal Security, MSST Honolulu conducts high-risk vessel escorts, enforces safety and security zones, and executes maritime pursuit operations to prevent illegal activity. Applying our unique authorities and capabilities, they are at the leading edge of our operations to control, secure, and defend U.S. borders and maritime approaches.

MSST Honolulu’s presence underscores the Coast Guard’s commitment to the Indo-Pacific, strengthens regional stability, and plays a key role in advancing U.S. interests in a dynamic and strategic region.
#USCG #MSSTHonolulu #MaritimeSecurity #IndoPacific

tweets.text:
Last month, the Coast Guard's Maritime Law Enforcement Academy celebrated its 20th anniversary in a ceremony in Charleston, SC. MLEA is the Coast Guard’s training center for all things law enforcement. Founded in December 2004, MLEA combined the premier boarding officer school and boarding team member school into one location to meet post-9/11 training requirements. In the two decades since, MLEA continues to play a vital role in enhancing security, safety, and operational effectiveness on the water.

The celebration brought together past and current members and civilians to pay tribute to the instructors and staff behind MLEA's success.

“Our instructors at the MLEA are driven by excellence and a deep commitment to developing the next generation of law enforcement professionals—equipping them with the skills, knowledge, and dedication needed to combat illicit drugs, address unlawful migration and safeguard America’s boating community,” said U.S. Coast Guard Lt. j.g. Daniel Jones, MLEA Basic Law Enforcement Branch Course Chief.

MLEA continues to uphold the service’s legacy of safeguarding the coasts, protecting the homeland, and preserving American lives. And Coast Guard law enforcement will continue to adapt to new challenges and demands while maintaining readiness and excellence.

#MLEA #USCG #LawEnforcement #CounterNarcotics

tweets.text:
#DYK that one of the Coast Guard’s primary missions is to keep America’s ports and waterways safe, secure, and protected?

With 361 international ports and approximately 25,000 miles of navigable waterways to guard, our marine inspectors utilize a detailed methodical process that involves assessing the integrity of containers, verifying documentation, and ensuring compliance with international safety standards. These inspections are an essential step in bringing goods to consumers while maintaining the integrity of our ports and waterways.

U.S. ports of entry are part of our nation’s Marine Transportation System network, and function as extensions of the U.S. border. Their safety is instrumental in ensuring the security of our nation and our economic prosperity.

#cgmts2025 #USCG #MaritimeTransportation #inspection #portsandwaterways

author.description: Official U.S. Coast Guard X account - The Nation’s premier maritime military service and law enforcement agency. If in distress, use VHF channel 16 or dial 911.

tweets.text:
Recently, the crew of the USCGC Elm got their hands (and the cutter’s deck) dirty helping out NOAA and the National Data Buoy Center with an inoperable weather buoy, in the waters outside of San Francisco, California.

Buoy Station 46026 had not been serviced in quite some time and due to the vast marine growth (estimated at 20,000lbs), the buoy was sitting incredibly low in the water. Amazingly though, Buoy Station 46026 was still reporting data until this last February!   

Weather buoys, such as 46026, provides a vital service for vessel operations across the United States.  From cargo ships to our nation’s vast fishing fleets and everything in between, weather buoys provide mariners essential information for safe maritime travel.

The U.S. Coast Guard regularly works with National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to assist with servicing weather buoys all over the country.

To find out more about the buoys in the United States, see the link below:

https://t.co/6SYRJfmpOq

#CGMTS2025 #USCG #Navigation #BuoyTender

tweets.text:
Earlier this month, a U.S. Coast Guard Station Miami Beach LE crew assisted CBP Air and Marine Operations and Homeland Security Investigations special agents in the interdiction of suspected smuggling vessel.

Working together, we seized 245 pounds of cocaine valued at approximately $3.4 million from a 52-foot sailing vessel from Colombia, on April 5.

The Coast Guard continues to increase operations to interdict, seize, and disrupt transshipment of cocaine and other bulk illicit drugs by sea. These drugs fuel and enable cartels and Transnational Criminal Organizations (TCO) to produce and traffic illegal fentanyl threatening the U.S.

Working alongside our interagency partners, the men and women of the Coast Guard are doing everything in their power to interdict drugs before they reach our shores and our citizens.

#Smuggler #USCG #Interdiction #DrugBust

author.description: Official U.S. Coast Guard X account - The Nation’s premier maritime military service and law enforcement agency. If in distress, use VHF channel 16 or dial 911.

tweets.text:
15 years ago, on April 20, 2010, the Deepwater Horizon (DWH) oil rig blew up.

Located 45 miles southeast of Venice, Louisiana, the explosion resulted in the tragic deaths of 11 workers and, within two days, the loss of the rig.

For the next 87 days, approximately 4 million barrels of oil flowed from the damaged Macondo well before it was finally capped on July 15, 2010.

Over 8,500 United States Coast Guard personnel were deployed in response to the Deepwater Horizon oil spill, including during the months of clean-up, fact-finding, and trials.

Today, we remember the lives lost while also thanking the thousands of volunteers who responded to help work alongside the local, state, and federal agencies who responded.

#USCG #DeepwaterHorizon #OilSpill #cleanup

tweets.text:
#USCG U.S. Coast Guard Cutter David Duren (WPC 1156) interdicted 19 aliens aboard a 25-foot cuddy cabin approximately 23 miles west of Oceanside, California, early Tuesday morning.

All passengers aboard the cuddy cabin stated they had not had food or water for two days and were medically evaluated with no major medical concerns.

The group-18 adult males and one adult female all claimed Mexican nationality.
All 19 were taken into custody by the Coast Guard and transferred to Border Patrol personnel in Oceanside. 
#USCG #SouthwestBorder

tweets.text:
🚢🇺🇸 Welcome Home, Crew of the U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Active! 🚢🇺🇸
After an intense 65-day law enforcement patrol off the coast of Southern California, the dedicated crew has returned to Port Angeles! 🌊✨

During this mission, the Active covered over 5,500 miles as part of the Coast Guard District 11’s Southwest Maritime Border Security operations. Their efforts focused on countering Transnational Criminal Organization activity and ensuring the safety and security of U.S. maritime borders.

👏 They successfully interdicted three vessels carrying 46 illegal aliens and provided assistance for the apprehension of another 40, all safely transferred to Customs and Border Protection in San Diego.
In addition to their law enforcement duties, they conducted crucial training exercises with regional Coast Guard crews, enhancing their military readiness. Their commitment to search and rescue was also on full display, as they responded to four cases while offshore northern California.

Thank you to the brave crew for their hard work and dedication! 💪🌟

Shown, USCGC Terrell Horne (WPC-1131) working with the USCGC Active.

📸: Petty Officer 2nd Class Brenton Kludt

Repost from @uscgpacarea

#USCoastGuard #ProtectingOurBorders #MaritimeSecurity #SemperParatus

tweets.text:
Thank you to everyone who has expressed concern for the wellbeing of our U.S. Coast Guard responders as they continue to work closely with our partners following the aircraft collision over the Potomac.

We could not complete our mission to bring closure for the families affected by this tragedy without your support.

The Coast Guard is dedicated to ensuring our members have 24/7 access to both Coast Guard Chaplains as well as our Critical Incident Stress Management (CISM) team members who are trained to provide Psychological First Aid during major incidents.

Thank you again and #SemperParatus

@uscgmidatlantic @uscglantarea #coastguard

tweets.text:
The #CoastGuard is uniquely positioned to combat Illegal, Unreported, and Unregulated Fishing (IUU-F) and uphold the law at sea. #IUUF undermines a nation's sovereignty, threatens economic security, and weakens global rules-based order. 🐟

Today we honor the memories of Lt. Cdmr. Dale Taylor, Lt. j.g. Thomas Cameron, ASTC Fernando Jorge, and AET3 Andrew Knight who were lost on a training sortie from #CoastGuard Aviation Training Center (ATC) Mobile, Alabama, Feb. 28, 2012.
